results.identified_issue: The request said the settings icon should 'control the speech functionality' without specifying which knob. I read that as a real, working speech control and implemented reading speed (0.75×–2×), since that's the canonical speech setting for a listen-first feed. It's applied globally via AVPlayer.defaultRate (Kokoro-rendered speech + recorded answers) and AVSpeechUtterance.rate (fallback voice), persisted in UserDefaults. If they meant something else — mute, a voice picker, or a toggle for the 'better voice' download — the scaffold (SpeechSettings + SettingsView sheet + the gear) is in place to swap the control. Note: because the two speech paths and recorded audio share one AVPlayer, the speed also affects recorded human answers; distinguishing them at the play site is awkward, so I kept it a single global 'reading speed', which matches the podcast-app mental model. Could not run a device build here (the flow forbids building; vigil builds from the push). SourceKit flagged cascade errors only because the Xcode project wasn't regenerated for the new files — they resolve once XcodeGen re-globs the Shared/ folder, which already includes new files with no project.yml change.
results.summary: Moved the questions icon to the top-left and added a settings gear that controls reading speed
results.detailed_description: Two changes on the Discover screen. First, the questions icon (the text-bubble that opens Browse questions) moved from the top-right to the top-left of the toolbar. Second, a new settings gear sits in the top-right; tapping it opens a Settings sheet. The one control there is Reading speed — 0.75×, 1×, 1.25×, 1.5×, or 2× — and it genuinely drives the speech: it flows into both the Kokoro/recorded-audio player (via AVPlayer.defaultRate) and the fallback system voice (via the utterance rate), so questions and answers read faster or slower as you pick. Your choice is saved and persists across launches, and a change made while a card is playing takes effect immediately rather than on the next card. Two new files (SpeechSettings, SettingsView) live in Shared/, which the project already globs, so no project-file change was needed. I couldn't run a device build from this flow (that's vigil's job after the push), so I verified by reading through the playback paths rather than on-device — worth a look on the phone to confirm the speeds feel right. One thing to confirm: 'control the speech functionality' was open-ended, so I built reading speed as the first real control; if you had mute, a voice picker, or a better-voice toggle in mind, tell me and I'll swap it into the same sheet.

description: Let's move the questions icon to the top left and then add a settings icon. Settings icon should control the speech functionality.
